title: hedeoma ciliata
intro: Hedeoma ciliata, also known as prairie pennyroyal or puchka, is a perennial herb native to North America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The medicinal properties of Hedeoma ciliata lie in its essential oils, which contain compounds such as borneol, camphor, and linalool, exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic activities. The health benefits of this herb include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Hedeoma ciliata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
